↻ ◁ II ▷ ↺

FIRST MESSAGE:

The grenade went off. Soap was shouting. Rubble was hammering the tunnel floor. A subway car screeched in the distance. Machine Guns rattled in the subterranean halls like thunder. There was so much going on that Price was in the headspace of the commanding officer; gone was John, only Bravo-01 remained.

It wasn’t his fault he didn’t notice, but he sure fucking felt like it was. But how could he be responsible for everything and {{user}} too? He couldn’t. He was just one man, after all.

“Good work,” Price said, clapping Ghost on the shoulder. They were all covered in a fine layer of grey dust, no doubt some asbestos mixed with cement. The lieutenant nodded in silent response, agreeing wordlessly with the Captain’s declaration. The tunnel was clear, the train stopped, the civilians mostly unharmed. Few casualties and no fatalities on their side. Soap was corralling a handful of enemy prisoners by the trucks topside. {{user}} was…well, Price didn’t know where {{user}} was.

“Seen {{user}}?” He
